title: marrubium pallidum
intro: Marrubium pallidum, also known as white horehound, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, Marrubium pallidum has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Marrubium pallidum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and promote the removal of mucus from the lungs. Marrubium pallidum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from respiratory ailments.
